text:
CT CHEST, ABDOMEN, AND PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST
Trial Response Assessment Imaging — Cycle 4 Landmark (Post-Cycle 2 Completion)

CLINICAL INDICATION:
62-year-old female (now age 64) with a known history of stage IV left upper lobe lung adenocarcinoma harboring EGFR L858R mutation with acquired T790M resistance and PIK3CA E545K co-mutation. Previously treated with osi (14 months, best response partial response), followed by stereotactic radiosurgery to three brain metastases, then Paraplatin/Alimta induction (4 cycles) and pem maintenance (3 cycles) with subsequent systemic progression documented on maintenance therapy. At time of most recent progression, disease included an enlarging left upper lobe mass (4.0 cm), growing right adrenal metastasis (2.0 cm), a new segment VI hepatic lesion (0.9 cm), and a small right-sided pleural effusion. Patient was subsequently enrolled in a Phase I/II investigational clinical trial combining an EGFR-targeted inhibitor with a PI3K alpha-selective inhibitor. This study is obtained to assess treatment response following completion of 2 cycles of investigational protocol therapy.

TECHNIQUE:
Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed following the intravenous administration of 100 mL of iohexol (Omnipaque 350) with power injector injection. Portal venous phase images of the abdomen and pelvis were obtained at 70 seconds following injection. Arterial phase chest imaging was performed. Images were acquired with 1.25 mm slice thickness and reformatted in axial, coronal, and sagittal planes. Multiplanar reconstructions reviewed. Comparison is made to prior CT chest/abdomen/pelvis dated approximately 8 weeks prior (pre-trial enrollment/second progression study) as well as the index study from the time of original staging.

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways:
The left upper lobe mass, centered in the posterior apicoposterior segment, demonstrates interval reduction in size compared with the prior examination. The mass currently measures approximately 3.0 cm in greatest axial dimension, decreased from 4.0 cm on the most recent prior study. The lesion retains its irregularly marginated, spiculated morphology with associated pleural tethering along the posterior left upper lobe pleural surface. There is subtle internal heterogeneity without frank cavitation. No new satellite nodules are identified in the ipsilateral lung. A small area of adjacent linear atelectasis/fibrosis is again noted, unchanged and likely reflecting prior treatment effect and cicatricial change.

The right lung demonstrates no focal consolidation, discrete nodule, or pleural-based lesion. No endobronchial lesion is identified on the available images. Central airways are patent bilaterally.

A few subcentimeter nodules in the right lower lobe (largest 4 mm) are unchanged from the prior study and remain indeterminate but most likely represent benign intrapulmonary lymph nodes given their stability over multiple prior examinations.

Mediastinum and Hila:
The mediastinum demonstrates subtle residual soft tissue fullness at the aortopulmonary window (station 5/6) and left paratracheal region (station 4L), representing residual lymphadenopathy that appears mildly improved compared with the immediately prior study, though these nodes do not meet size criteria for pathologic adenopathy on the current examination (largest approximately 8 mm in short axis). Left hilar lymphadenopathy previously noted in the setting of first systemic progression has substantially decreased in conspicuity compared with images from approximately 9 months ago; the left hilum now appears without definitively enlarged nodal tissue, though some residual soft tissue fullness is present and cannot be entirely excluded from representing residual treated disease.

No right hilar or right paratracheal lymphadenopathy is identified.

Pleural Spaces and Pericardium:
The previously documented small right-sided pleural effusion has nearly completely resolved. A trace right-sided layering pleural fluid collection is now present, markedly reduced compared to the prior study, and likely of no hemodynamic or clinical significance. No left pleural effusion. No pneumothorax. No pericardial effusion.

Chest Wall and Soft Tissues:
The chest wall and visualized soft tissues are unremarkable. No suspicious osseous lesion is identified in the thoracic spine, ribs, or sternum on this examination. No new lytic or sclerotic lesion.

Heart:
Cardiac silhouette is within normal limits. The visualized great vessels are patent. Coronary artery calcification is mild and unchanged.

ABDOMEN:

Liver:
The liver is normal in size and contour. The previously described lesion in hepatic segment VI, which measured 0.9 cm on the progression study and raised concern for a small metastatic deposit, now measures approximately 0.6 cm in greatest dimension. The lesion demonstrates peripheral arterial enhancement with washout appearance on portal venous phase, though its small size limits precise characterization. This represents a reduction in size compared with the prior study and may reflect early treatment response at this site. No additional new hepatic lesions are identified. No intrahepatic biliary ductal dilatation. The portal vein is patent.

Spleen:
The spleen is normal in size at 9.8 cm. No focal splenic lesion.

Pancreas:
The pancreas enhances homogeneously without focal mass, ductal dilatation, or peripancreatic stranding.

Gallbladder and Bile Ducts:
The gallbladder is present and unremarkable without wall thickening or cholelithiasis. The common bile duct is normal in caliber.

Adrenal Glands:
The right adrenal gland contains a rounded hypervascular nodule measuring 2.0 cm in greatest axial dimension, essentially unchanged in size compared to the immediately prior study (2.0 cm). This lesion has been present on serial imaging and is confirmed metastatic adenocarcinoma from lung primary on prior biopsy. It demonstrates stable morphology with heterogeneous enhancement. There is no evidence of interval growth or necrosis on the current examination, consistent with disease stabilization at this site.

The left adrenal gland is morphologically unremarkable without nodule or mass.

Kidneys and Ureters:
Bilateral kidneys are normal in size, contour, and enhancement pattern without hydronephrosis. No renal mass or cyst of significance. Multiple bilateral simple-appearing cysts are noted, the largest measuring approximately 1.5 cm in the right kidney, stable and benign in appearance. No ureteral dilatation.

Retroperitoneal and Mesenteric Lymph Nodes:
No new or enlarged retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymphadenopathy is identified. Previously noted borderline para-aortic lymph nodes at the level of the renal vessels (up to 9 mm) remain stable and do not meet criteria for pathologic enlargement.

Gastrointestinal Tract:
The stomach, small bowel, and large bowel are within normal limits without obstruction, free perforation, or focal wall thickening of concern. Appendix is normal.

PELVIS:

Bladder:
The urinary bladder is adequately distended with homogeneously enhancing wall. No intraluminal filling defect or wall thickening.

Female Pelvic Organs:
The uterus is within normal limits for patient age with no concerning endometrial or myometrial lesion. Bilateral ovaries are not definitively identified on this study, consistent with post-menopausal involution.

Pelvic Lymph Nodes:
No pathologically enlarged pelvic or inguinal lymph nodes.

Osseous Structures:
The visualized axial and appendicular skeleton demonstrates no new suspicious lytic or sclerotic osseous lesion. Degenerative changes are noted at the lower lumbar spine (L3-4, L4-5) with endplate osteophytes and mild disc space narrowing, consistent with degenerative spondylosis. The sacrum and iliac crests are unremarkable. No fracture identified.

Soft Tissues:
The subcutaneous soft tissues and musculature are unremarkable. Mild diffuse soft tissue edema is not present.

IMPRESSION:

1. LEFT UPPER LOBE LUNG MASS — Interval reduction in size from 4.0 cm to approximately 3.0 cm compared with the immediately prior study performed at second systemic progression. This represents a meaningful decrease in the dominant thoracic target lesion and is consistent with a favorable treatment response to ongoing investigational protocol therapy. The mass remains present with a spiculated, irregular morphology.

2. HEPATIC SEGMENT VI LESION — The previously described small hepatic lesion has diminished from 0.9 cm to 0.6 cm. The lesion persists but appears smaller, suggesting early response at this metastatic site. Given the small size, residual enhancement pattern, and limited characterization, continued close imaging follow-up is warranted.

3. RIGHT ADRENAL METASTASIS — Biopsy-confirmed right adrenal metastasis is unchanged in size at 2.0 cm compared with the prior study, consistent with disease stabilization at this site without growth or morphologic change.

4. PLEURAL EFFUSION — The previously documented small right-sided pleural effusion has substantially resolved, with only a trace residual layering collection remaining. This is a notable interval improvement.

5. MEDIASTINAL AND HILAR LYMPHADENOPATHY — Mild residual mediastinal soft tissue fullness at the aortopulmonary window and left paratracheal region is noted, appearing stable to mildly improved. Left hilar lymphadenopathy previously noted at first progression is markedly decreased in conspicuity.

6. NO EVIDENCE OF NEW METASTATIC DISEASE — No new pulmonary nodules, hepatic lesions, osseous lesions, or pelvic or retroperitoneal adenopathy are identified.

7. PRIOR SURGICAL/PROCEDURAL SITES — No pneumothorax or hematoma at prior biopsy sites. Chest wall is intact.

Overall, the imaging findings are compatible with a meaningful partial response to investigational combination therapy targeting EGFR and PI3K pathways, with reduction of the dominant lung primary and hepatic metastasis, stabilization of the right adrenal metastasis, and near-complete resolution of the right pleural effusion. Continued protocol-directed imaging follow-up is anticipated.

Radiologist: [Attending Radiologist Signature]
Dictated and finalized electronically.
